$comment: Phase 6 (TODO-07) GitHub Issues MANIFEST. LOCAL ONLY - no gh/API call was made. Ready to create later after user approval. One issue per Sprint-1 story (11). Source: backlog.md, sprint_1_backlog.md, SRS.md. Target repo: techdeveloper-org (existing). DO NOT create until user approves.

milestones.title: Sprint 1
milestones.description: Foundation thin-slice + mandated data-handling compliance substrate. Goal: deployable, authenticated, tenant-isolated ingestion-and-routing slice (upload -> TOC -> chunk -> embed -> store -> route) with DPDP x-pii access. 11 issues, 39 SP committed vs 39.9 SP ceiling (42 SP capacity).

issues.title: [STORY-001] PostgreSQL structure schema + migrations
issues.body:
## Story STORY-001

**Epic:** EPIC-STO
**As a** backend engineer **I want** the documents and sections tables (L1/L2 hierarchy, tenant_id, page_start/page_end, summary) with versioned migrations **so that** the router and document-management surfaces have an ACID structure store (ADR-10).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-003, FR-024
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-003, AC-024

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-003, AC-024) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 8.0
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 3

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- Forward + rollback migrations. snake_case tables/columns. tenant_id on every row. Postgres is the structure store (ADR-10); Qdrant holds chunk vectors separately.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-038] Auth: API keys (rotation) + OAuth2/JWT (personal) + tenant substrate
issues.body:
## Story STORY-038

**Epic:** EPIC-SEC
**As a** platform owner **I want** API-key auth with rotation for the enterprise API and OAuth2/JWT bearer for the personal tool, plus the tenant_id scoping path **so that** both product surfaces are authenticated and tenant isolation is enforceable (ADR-7, SRS-NFR-015).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** NFR-015
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-024

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-024) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 6.0
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 5

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- API-key rotation; JWT validation; rate-limit on authenticated ops (ADV-003); session-fixation review hook; audit-logging substrate begins here (CERT-In/IT Act). No secrets in repo.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-002] Qdrant collection + payload schema (chunk vectors)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-002

**Epic:** EPIC-STO
**As a** data engineer **I want** the Qdrant collection provisioned with payload {chunk_id, section_id, doc_id, page, tenant_id} and a section_id/tenant_id payload index **so that** targeted retrieval can filter by section and tenant (ADR-2).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-004
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-004

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-004) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 5.3
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 3

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- Payload index on section_id and tenant_id. dev=ChromaDB / prod=Qdrant parity documented.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-004] Service skeleton: Dockerfile + /health + /ready
issues.body:
## Story STORY-004

**Epic:** EPIC-OPS
**As a** devops engineer **I want** each service to ship a Dockerfile, a liveness /health, and a dependency-reachability /ready **so that** the platform is deployable and degradation is observable (SRS-NFR-009).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** NFR-009
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** NFR-009 (HLD 9)

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (NFR-009 (HLD 9)) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 7.3
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=1 / M=2 / P=4 -> E=2.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 2

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- /ready returns 503 when a dependency is down (feeds NFR-013 degradation UI).
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-005] CI pipeline: lint + type + unit + SAST + secrets scan
issues.body:
## Story STORY-005

**Epic:** EPIC-OPS
**As a** devops engineer **I want** a CI workflow running lint, type-check, unit tests, SAST, and a secrets scan on every push **so that** the quality + security gates block bad merges (AGREED CONTRACT devops + sast/secrets).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** NFR-007, NFR-010
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** CI blocks on hardcoded key

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (CI blocks on hardcoded key) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 7.7
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=1 / M=2 / P=5 -> E=2.3
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 2

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- SAST + secrets scan block on finding. Python + TS stacks. RFC-7807 contract test hook.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-008] PDF upload + text/structure extraction (POST /v1/documents)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-008

**Epic:** EPIC-ING
**As a** user **I want** to upload a PDF and have its text + structure extracted **so that** I can ask questions over it; identical re-upload returns the same doc_id (deduplicated).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-001
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-001

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-001) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 7.0
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 3

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- IngestResponse{doc_id, toc, ingest_status, deduplicated}. Idempotent on content hash. ADV-001 traceability header fix in first doc pass.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-009] Native TOC extraction (Scenario A, get_toc)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-009

**Epic:** EPIC-ING
**As a** user **I want** the native bookmark TOC extracted with level/title/page ranges **so that** the router has the document structure to route over (ADR-4, Scenario A).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-002
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-002

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 5.4
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=3 / P=6 -> E=3.3
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 3

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- Bookmark path only this sprint; pseudo-TOC (Scenario B/C) is STORY-010 in Sprint 2.

issues.title: [STORY-003] Embedding + upsert pipeline (OpenAI te-3-small)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-003

**Epic:** EPIC-STO
**As a** data engineer **I want** chunk embedding (text-embedding-3-small) and idempotent Qdrant upsert keyed on content hash **so that** re-upload reuses doc_id and chunks are indexed exactly once (ADR-6).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-004
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-004, AC-001

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-004, AC-001) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 5.3
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 5

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- Idempotent on content hash. Embedding provider key via env only.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-011] Section-aware 3-level chunking (no cross-section chunks)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-011

**Epic:** EPIC-ING
**As a** data engineer **I want** Document->Section->Chunk chunking where no chunk crosses a section boundary **so that** every chunk carries exactly one section_id for clean targeted retrieval.

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-003
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-003

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-003) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 5.5
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=4 / P=7 -> E=4.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 5

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- P0-RISK. Deterministic golden fixtures. No chunk spans two sections.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-014] LangGraph router node (Claude 3 Haiku, RouterOutput contract)
issues.body:
## Story STORY-014

**Epic:** EPIC-RTR
**As a** AI engineer **I want** a LangGraph router emitting {relevant_sections[], page_ranges[], confidence[], fallback, routing_time_ms} validated against the RouterOutput contract **so that** queries are scoped to sections before search (ADR-1/ADR-3, HLD 7.1).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-005, NFR-008
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-005

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-005) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 5.8
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=3 / M=5 / P=9 -> E=5.3
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 5

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- P0-RISK. Golden TOC fixtures. LLM response rejected if non-conforming -> deterministic fallback (ADV-004, OWASP-LLM01). routing_time_ms in [100,300] (SRS-NFR-001).
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.

issues.title: [STORY-034] DPDP access: GET /v1/documents/{id}/data + x-pii inventory
issues.body:
## Story STORY-034

**Epic:** EPIC-SEC
**As a** data subject **I want** GET /v1/documents/{id}/data to return the personal data held plus an x-pii field inventory **so that** my right to access (DPDP Section 8) and PII transparency (DPDP Section 4) are honored (HLD 7.4).

### Linked requirements
- **SRS-FR / NFR:** FR-026, FR-029
- **Acceptance criteria (SRS):** AC-026, AC-029

### Acceptance criteria
This story is accepted when the SRS acceptance criterion (AC-026, AC-029) passes with the global DoD satisfied. See `SRS.md` Section 4 for the exact AC text.

### Estimation
- **WSJF (CoD / Job-Size):** 6.0
- **PERT 3-point (ideal-days):** O=2 / M=3 / P=5 -> E=3.2
- **Story Points (Fibonacci):** 3

### Definition of Done
Global DoD (see `docs/phase-6-sprint-planning/backlog.md` Section 1.3) plus:
- AUTO-CREATED into Sprint 1 (data-handling). DataAccessExport.pii_fields[] enumerates x-pii fields; x-pii annotated in schema consumption.
- Story -> SRS-FR -> AC traceability recorded in `fr_story_traceability.json`.

> Source of truth: SRS.md, openapi.yaml (do-not-modify), HLD, AGREED CONTRACTS. This issue body is a MANIFEST entry generated in Phase 6 - not yet created on GitHub.
